acceptance_targets: [CH08_CRAFT_01] Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:131-147 contains Vess's failed retrieval and emotional cutoff without an immediate follow-up sentence that merely labels the phenomenon in abstract terms, unless that follow-up adds a new fact or decision. Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:223-245 moves from Maren naming the System into either a new concrete consequence or choice, or the final staying beat; fail if two consecutive paragraphs in that span still only paraphrase that the memories were deliberately removed.

[CH08_PROSE_01] Pass if, when reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:67-81 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:191-217, no more than one emotional beat is interpreted through construction or architecture language and the reader can still tell both Vess and Aldric are destabilized from the dialogue breaks and physical context alone. Fail if both passages still rely on repeated terms or equivalents like load-bearing, construction, building, structural wrongness, or other explicit voice-diagnosis formulas to explain the same effect.
[R-02] Chapter 8's opening three pages contain zero uses of the phrase 'eleven years' and no expository explanation of how System-sighted perception differs from Maren's.
[R-12] Line 1930 does not re-characterize Thessa's delivery style through a clinical/medical/infrastructure simile.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:131-147 contains Vess's failed retrieval and emotional cutoff without an immediate follow-up sentence that merely labels the phenomenon in abstract terms, unless that follow-up adds a new fact or decision. Pass if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:223-245 moves from Maren naming the System into either a new concrete consequence or choice, or the final staying beat; fail if two consecutive paragraphs in that span still only paraphrase that the memories were deliberately removed.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly translates its own revelations into diagnosis after the scene has already made them legible. Vess's missing-name beat lands at line 131, but line 133 immediately abstracts it into "selective absence"; Aldric's missing-death beat lands by lines 215-223, but lines 225-227 paraphrase the same inference again before the chapter moves into Maren's new responsibility. That self-explanation softens the eerie force of discovery and makes the quiet chapter feel more reported than turned.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the aftermath around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:131-147 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:223-245 so the chapter trusts the fractures already on the page. Either cut the immediate diagnostic paraphrase or replace it with new dramatic work: a concrete implication Maren has to act on, a sharper relational beat with Vess, Aldric, or Derry, or a choice that shows responsibility instead of restating it. If Aldric names the pattern, that line needs to add information Maren could not already infer from the failed name retrieval or missing anger; if the chapter keeps the post-"The System" passage, it should introduce a new cost, plan, or fear rather than another abstract summary of removed memory.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, when reading snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:67-81 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:191-217, no more than one emotional beat is interpreted through construction or architecture language and the reader can still tell both Vess and Aldric are destabilized from the dialogue breaks and physical context alone. Fail if both passages still rely on repeated terms or equivalents like load-bearing, construction, building, structural wrongness, or other explicit voice-diagnosis formulas to explain the same effect.
findings.problem: The narration falls back on the same nonliteral architecture and voice-annotation scaffold often enough to read as autopilot: "load-bearing," "construction," "building," "a man of complete sentences," and "the voice of a..." all arrive while the chapter is trying to show destabilized speech. Because the same template governs both Vess's and Aldric's disclosures, the prose starts explaining cadence instead of letting pause, interruption, and bodily detail do the work.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the interpretive narration in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:67-81 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:191-217. Keep at most one architecture or construction metaphor where it directly serves the System material, and replace the rest with Maren-locked perception: the actual pause length, a change in breath, the hum, hand pressure on stone, Derry's glance, or the way a sentence breaks. Cut slogan-like tags such as the stand-alone characterization at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:205 unless the revised line produces a sharper, less template-driven image.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 8's opening three pages contain zero uses of the phrase 'eleven years' and no expository explanation of how System-sighted perception differs from Maren's.
findings.evidence: Ch8 line 1916: 'faking his way across Fenhold's farmland'; line 1928: 'Eleven years of contamination in named villages, children growing up in the residue of quests he'd only pretended to clear'; line 1952: 'Eleven years of performing a System role'; line 1926: 'The difference was that the System-sighted saw it with data overlaid — quest details, threat levels, completion percentages — and Maren saw the raw thing.' System-blindness formula near-identical to Ch4 line 868. Adjacent to Ch7 which contains 5 'eleven years' uses.
findings.problem: Ch8 opening bundles three redundant baselines: (1) the fraud premise at line 1916, (2) 'eleven years' twice at lines 1928 and 1952, and (3) the System-blindness perceptual formula at line 1926 restating the 'everyone sees data, Maren sees raw light' contrast established at Ch4 line 868. All three are well-established by Ch8.
findings.rewrite_direction: Line 1916: replace the fraud-recap clause with a sensory detail about the road. Line 1926: cut the explanatory contrast ('The difference was that the System-sighted saw it with data overlaid...'); by Ch8 the reader understands Maren's blindness. Line 1928: keep the guilt-weight but cut 'Eleven years of contamination in named villages' — replace with a specific image. Line 1952: cut 'Eleven years of performing a System role.'

findings.acceptance_test: Line 1930 does not re-characterize Thessa's delivery style through a clinical/medical/infrastructure simile.
findings.evidence: Ch8 line 1930: 'Thessa had said it the way she said everything — clinically, as if the fact of his approaching dissolution was a drainage problem and she was describing the pipe.' Ch7 line 1661: 'clipped, clinical — a surgeon cataloguing damage.' Adjacent chapters.
findings.problem: Thessa's clinical voice is re-described in Ch8 (line 1930) with a medical/infrastructure simile, adjacent to Ch7 where it was first established with a parallel simile at line 1661. Both describe the same trait: Thessa delivers devastating information in detached professional register.
findings.rewrite_direction: At line 1930, cut 'the way she said everything — clinically, as if...' and let the quoted content convey Thessa's delivery. The reader met Thessa one chapter ago.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
